findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is missing in the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the North Carolina state seal between the header text and 'NORTH CAROLINA Environmental Quality'. The HTML rendering does not include this seal image.

findings.description: The DEQ logo at the bottom of page 1 is missing in the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has the DEQ logo in the footer area. The HTML version omits this logo but retains the footer text.

findings.description: The handwritten signature of David Miller is missing in the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a handwritten signature above 'David Miller, PE'. The HTML version has no signature image.

findings.description: Page 2 of the HTML includes both an inverted/upside-down image of the original PDF page and a right-side-up text transcription, creating duplicate content
findings.evidence: The HTML page 2 shows an upside-down scan image of the letter at the top, followed by the same content rendered as HTML text below it. The PDF page 2 is simply the back side of the page (showing through/upside down).

findings.description: The handwritten signatures of Brian Wrenn and Brenda Harris are missing in the HTML rendering of the Notice of Issuance
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows handwritten signatures for Brian Wrenn (Director) and Brenda Harris. The HTML rendering on page 4 omits these signatures.

findings.description: Page 4 of the HTML includes both an inverted/upside-down image of the Notice of Issuance and a right-side-up text transcription, creating duplicate content
findings.evidence: The HTML page 4 shows an upside-down scan at the top followed by the same content as readable HTML text below.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create structural duplication. The outer <main> wraps an inner <main>, which is incorrect HTML structure.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <main><main>...</main></main> - two nested main elements.

findings.description: The 'NOTICE OF ISSUANCE OF MINING PERMIT' is rendered as h1, but the document already has page 1 content preceding it. Using h1 for a second-page section heading is acceptable as a document title choice, but the underline styling via <u> tag is presentational. More importantly, there is no h1 for the cover letter portion (page 1), creating an inconsistent heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
HTML:

NOTICE OF ISSUANCE OF
MINING PERMIT

— this is the only h1 in the document, applied to page 2 content.

findings.description: Alt text for scanned page images is generic rather than descriptive of actual content.
findings.evidence: alt='Scanned document page 1' and alt='Scanned document page 2' — these are generic descriptions rather than describing the actual content (e.g., a letter from NC DEQ or a mining permit notice).
